{
  "analysis": "The original /app/mystery has been run and image.ppm exists: P3 ASCII 800x600 maxval 255, size 5362624 bytes. The program prints fixed status messages and ignores stdin. We have gcc, objdump, nm, readelf, python3, gzip, wc but no file/xxd/hexdump/strace/gdb/ffmpeg. Next we need to reverse-engineer or infer the exact pixel algorithm and confirm it is unaffected by args/env, then implement /app/mystery.c. We should preserve the original image and inspect symbols/disassembly/pixel patterns.",
  "plan": "Copy image.ppm as reference. Check whether arguments/environment affect output by running the binary with args and in another directory if possible. Inspect symbols with nm/readelf. Disassemble around main with objdump and grep. Parse image.ppm with Python to sample pixels and compute statistics, which should reveal whether the generator is a simple gradient/fractal/pattern.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "cp image.ppm /tmp/original.ppm
",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "mkdir -p /tmp/run2 && cd /tmp/run2 && /app/mystery arg1 arg2 >out.txt 2>err.txt; echo "exit=$?"; cmp /tmp/original.ppm image.ppm && echo "same image with args"; cmp -s /tmp/run2/out.txt /dev/stdin <<'EOF'
Creating high-resolution image: 800x600
This will be downsampled with ffmpeg after rendering
Rendering high-resolution image...
Progress: 99.8%
Rendering complete
Wrote image to image.ppm
Done.
EOF
echo "stdout same with args"; cat /tmp/run2/err.txt
",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "nm -C /app/mystery | head -200
",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "readelf -h /app/mystery; readelf -s /app/mystery | head -120
",
      "duration": 0.1
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "objdump -d /app/mystery | grep -A120 "<main>"
",
      "duration": 1.0
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
data=Path('/tmp/original.ppm').read_text().split()
assert data[0]=='P3'
w=int(data[1]); h=int(data[2]); maxv=int(data[3])
vals=list(map(int,data[4:]))
print(w,h,maxv,vals[:12], len(vals), w*h*3)
px=lambda x,y:(vals[(y*w+x)*3],vals[(y*w+x)*3+1],vals[(y*w+x)*3+2])
for y in [0,1,2,299,300,301,598,599]:
    for x in [0,1,2,399,400,401,798,799]:
        print(x,y,px(x,y))
print('row0 first 30', [px(x,0) for x in range(30)])
print('col0 first 30', [px(0,y) for y in range(30)])
print('center 25', [px(400+x,300+y) for y in range(-2,3) for x in range(-2,3)])
print('unique r',len(set(vals[0::3])),'unique g',len(set(vals[1::3])),'unique b',len(set(vals[2::3])))
print('minmax',min(vals),max(vals))
PY
",
      "duration": 1.0
    }
  ]
}
